description: We are the digital centre of government. We serve the public, central government departments and the wider public sector. GDS is part of the Department for Science, Innovation and Technology .

details.body:
We are the digital centre of government. We serve the public, central government departments and the wider public sector.

GDS is part of the Department for Science, Innovation and Technology.

image.alt_text: A blue circle contains the initialism API. Arrows from the circle point to illustrations representing the GOV.UK website, the GOV.UK app, services on GOV.UK, GOV.UK Chat, GOV.UK Search and GOV.UK Agents

ordered_featured_documents.summary: We’re exploring how the application programming interfaces (APIs) that deliver content for GOV.UK could evolve to better support a multi-channel experience. Find out what we’re doing and why we want to understand more about our users.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Why GOV.UK APIs are changing and how you can get involved

image.alt_text: Three rounded rectangle shapes against a dark blue background. The shapes contain the text "Learnings from Chat"

ordered_featured_documents.summary: Learn about the key findings from our pilots of GOV.UK Chat, and how we’ve evaluated its performance ahead of the next phase.
ordered_featured_documents.title: 5 things we learned testing GOV.UK Chat: an AI assistant for government

image.alt_text: A computer screen showing the numerals 2025, with a phone screen displaying the GOV.UK crown, graphs, social media engagement icons and a magnifying glass against a blue background.

ordered_featured_documents.summary: User insights help us to understand how we can develop GOV.UK to ensure it continues to meet people’s needs and work well for everyone. Learn about some of 2025’s main findings across the GOV.UK website, app and social media channels.
ordered_featured_documents.title: How people used GOV.UK in 2025

image.alt_text: Liz Kendall, Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, presenting data on large screens to an attentive audience in a modern conference room. The setting is professional and engaging.

ordered_featured_documents.summary: Our roadmap for modern digital government sets out how teams across government are transforming public services through joined‑up platforms, AI adoption, stronger digital infrastructure, investment in talent, smarter funding, and greater transparency.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Our roadmap for modern digital government

image.alt_text: A woman using a mobile phone sitting in front of a laptop computer. A speech bubble says "Improving government services with CustomerFirst"

ordered_featured_documents.summary: The government is launching CustomerFirst, a new unit within the Department for Science, Innovation and Technology, to modernise public services and improve the experience for millions of people who rely on them.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Government customer services to be modernised with help of industry experts

image.alt_text: Graphic showing 3 mobile phone screens with GOV.UK app on them. Title "GOV.UK Public beta"

ordered_featured_documents.summary: The GOV.UK app went live in public beta in July 2025. Find out what’s been happening and what’s coming next.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Insights from the first 5 months of the GOV.UK app public beta

details.secondary_corporate_information_pages: Our Personal information charter explains how we treat your personal information.

details.body:
The officer leads the government’s technology strategy by:

  • ensuring security and architectural alignment
  • managing technical risks
  • overseeing Engineering and Architecture functions to enhance digital capabilities and achieve world-leading digital government ambitions

role_appointments.title: David Knott - Government Chief Technology Officer

details.body:
The director is responsible for the:

  • delivery of the National Data Library
  • responsible policy and coordination of public sector geospatial data assets
  • governance of public sector geospatial data assets, including the delivery of the National Underground Asset Register

role.title: Director, National Data Library and Geospatial

role_appointments.title: Sarah Hodgetts - Director, National Data Library and Geospatial

details.body:
Responsibilities include:

  • Providing Executive representation on the Geospatial Commission’s independent board
  • Accountability for the Geospatial Commission’s multi-million annual budget
  • Responsibility for effective programme delivery and data contract management
  • Oversight of strategy development, prioritisation and implementation
  • Engagement at senior levels to represent the Geospatial Commission and promote its interests

The Government Chief Data Officer is responsible for fostering a sustainable, data-centric culture across government and the wider public sector.

They also lead the development and implementation of the government-wide data strategy, promote common data standards, and enhance data sharing across departments and the Government Data and Digital (GDaD) community.

role_appointments.title: Aimee Smith - Government Chief Data Officer

details.body: The Director General for Digital Products Group is responsible for the strategic product direction and operational excellence of government products and services. They ensure the delivery of world-class digital services that transform the way citizens interact with the government.

role.title: Interim Director General, Digital Products Group

role_appointments.title: Christine Bellamy - (Interim) Director General, Digital Products Group

details.body: The officer is responsible for the strategic product direction and operational excellence of government products and services. They ensure the delivery of world-class digital services that transform the way citizens interact with the government.

role_appointments.title: Christine Bellamy - Government Chief Product Officer

details.body: The CEO is responsible for the strategic direction, and operational excellence of GDS. They ensure the delivery of world-class digital services that transform the way citizens interact with the government.

role.title: Chief Executive Officer, Government Digital Service

role_appointments.title: Christine Bellamy - Chief Executive Officer, Government Digital Service

details.body: The Director of GOV.UK has responsibility for GOV.UK, the best place to find government services and information. GOV.UK is run and maintained by the Government Digital Service.

The Director General is responsible for catalysing cross-government digital transformation from within the Government Digital Service. This includes digital strategy, performance and assurance, service transformation, the public sector adoption of AI, plus the foundations that enable transformation such as data, technical architecture, the service manual, and digital skills and capability in the civil service.

role.title: Interim Director General, Digital Transformation Group

role_appointments.title: Emily Middleton - Interim Director General, Digital Transformation Group

details.body: The (temporary) Director General is responsible for the Digital Centre Design project, designing the scope, strategy and structure of the new digital centre of government. This is part of DSIT’s overall organisational transformation following the Machinery of Government (MoG) change of 24 July.

role_appointments.title: Emily Middleton - Director General, Digital Centre Design

details.description: We're unable to respond promptly to letters sent in the post at the moment. Please email to ensure we can respond to your enquiry as quickly as possible.

details.body: The Executive Director leads the Government Digital Service and is Head of Function for Digital, Data and Technology. The Executive Director is also a member of the Cabinet Office’s Executive Committee.
The Director General leads the Government Digital Service and is Head of Function for Digital, Data and Technology. The Director General is also a member of the Cabinet Office’s Executive Committee.
The Director General leads the Government Digital Service and is Head of Function for Digital, Data and Technology. The Director General is also a member of the Cabinet Office’s Executive Committee.

ordered_roles.title: Interim Director General, Government Digital Service

details.body: The CEO is responsible for the strategic direction, and operational excellence of GDS. They ensure the delivery of world-class digital services that transform the way citizens interact with the government.

ordered_roles.title: Chief Executive Officer, Government Digital Service

details.body: The Chief Product and Technology Officer for the Government Digital Service (GDS) is responsible for the digital professions at GDS as well as our Digital Service Platforms and Software Engineering.

The Chief Operating Officer is responsible for the day-to-day running and ongoing transformation of the Government Digital Service (GDS). They lead the HR, strategy and planning, communication, finance, commercial, information assurance and governance functions, and ensure the organisation is high performing and able to meet its objectives.

The officer provides leadership to the Government Digital and Data function across government and is responsible for leading the government’s digital transformation.

They also lead the Government Digital and Data function for the government by putting the right conditions and capabilities in place to enable departments to transform through digital and technology.

The (temporary) Director General is responsible for the Digital Centre Design project, designing the scope, strategy and structure of the new digital centre of government. This is part of DSIT’s overall organisational transformation following the Machinery of Government (MoG) change of 24 July.

The officer is responsible for the strategic product direction and operational excellence of government products and services. They ensure the delivery of world-class digital services that transform the way citizens interact with the government.

The officer leads the government’s technology strategy by:

  • ensuring security and architectural alignment
  • managing technical risks
  • overseeing Engineering and Architecture functions to enhance digital capabilities and achieve world-leading digital government ambitions

The Government Chief Data Officer is responsible for fostering a sustainable, data-centric culture across government and the wider public sector.

They also lead the development and implementation of the government-wide data strategy, promote common data standards, and enhance data sharing across departments and the Government Data and Digital (GDaD) community.

The director is responsible for the:

  • delivery of the National Data Library
  • responsible policy and coordination of public sector geospatial data assets
  • governance of public sector geospatial data assets, including the delivery of the National Underground Asset Register

ordered_roles.title: Director, National Data Library and Geospatial

They also lead the Government Digital and Data function for the government by putting the right conditions and capabilities in place to enable departments to transform through digital and technology.
